assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items discuss the U.S.-Iran war, Trump signaling possible exit, and market hopes for an end, but there is no clear evidence of both (1) a U.S. public confirmation and (2) an Iranian government/IRGC-aligned public confirmation of a bilateral ceasefire/pause with an effective date/time.

News references concern about '$4 gas' and energy disruptions, but the prediction requires AAA reporting the U.S. national average regular gasoline price at or above $4.25/gal in at least one daily reading; no AAA $4.25+ national-average reading is provided here.

No IMF WEO/WEO Update tables or Euro Area 2026 growth forecast revisions are cited in today’s news bullets, so there is no clear confirmation that a ≥0.3pp downward revision has occurred by the April 30 deadline.
There is no ECB policy-rate decision or Deposit Facility Rate change reported in today’s bullets; the deadline is 2026-09-30 and cannot be assessed yet.

Today’s coverage includes broad equity-market moves and volatility commentary but does not establish the EURO STOXX 50 level relative to its 2026-03-31 close or whether it has closed ≥10% below that reference.
No reporting here indicates the FTC/DOJ issued a formal Second Request (or equivalent compulsory information request) tied to Sysco’s referenced acquisition; deadline is 2026-08-31.

Today’s DOJ-related items concern litigation and agency response issues, not publication of new/updated nationwide DOJ guidance on law-enforcement facial recognition with explicit safeguards; no such guidance is mentioned.
No announcement is cited regarding Mistral AI’s Paris-area data center project (construction start or grid-connection agreement) or disclosure of planned IT load capacity ≥50MW.
There is no mention of Rebellions publicly filing an IPO prospectus/registration document with a securities regulator; deadline is 2026-12-31.
No Nature or Science publication about the referenced 'mysterious explosion' observed by JWST is indicated in today’s news bullets; deadline is 2026-12-31.
